name: United Midwest Savings Bank, National Association
description_short: Full-service national bank offering business lending, consumer loans, residential mortgages, and deposit products since 1889 with competitive CD rates and community focus.
description_long:
United Midwest Savings Bank, National Association (NMLS #516627) has operated since 1889, evolving from a community-focused institution into a national bank while maintaining personalized service standards. The bank positions itself as combining "the feel of a small community bank" with "the reach of a national brand," emphasizing ethical operations and customer-centric values throughout its 130+ year history.

The bank's core service offerings span four pillars: Business Lending (SBA loans under $150,000 and commercial financing), Consumer Lending (personal loans and credit products), Residential Lending (home mortgages and refinancing), and Deposit Services (checking, savings, and Certificates of Deposit). Current promotional rates include 12-month CDs at 4.00% APY. They offer CardValet mobile app functionality for card management and maintain 24/7 customer support through multiple phone channels (800-686-2052 for business hours, 800-472-3272 for after-hours).

The bank distinguishes itself through specific consumer protections and community commitments: AARP BankSafe Trained certification (renewed recognition) for protecting older Americans from financial exploitation, active fraud prevention education (check fraud awareness), and stated focus on financial education and small business development. Leadership emphasizes expertise and relationship-driven service across all lending categories.

As a traditional full-service bank, United Midwest Savings Bank serves customers seeking comprehensive banking relationships rather than specialized solutions. The primary limitation is that it functions as a conventional regional/national bank without the fintech conveniences of neobanks or the specialized focus of alternative lenders. Borrowers seeking SBA loans above $150,000 or those prioritizing digital-first banking may find limitations.
services: Business lending and financing (SBA loans under $150,000)
Consumer personal loans and credit products
Residential mortgage lending (home purchase and refinance)
Certificates of Deposit (multiple term lengths at competitive rates)
CardValet mobile app with card controls and fraud reporting
Check fraud protection education and prevention
pros: AARP BankSafe Trained certification with renewed recognition for elder fraud protection
Competitive CD rates (4.00% APY on 12-month terms) with flexible term options
24/7 customer support available at two dedicated phone numbers for different service needs
SBA lending expertise with loans available under $150,000
CardValet mobile app integration for real-time card controls and lost/stolen reporting
130+ years of operating history (since 1889) with stated commitment to ethical practices
Multi-channel support including local branches, phone, and mobile app services
cons: SBA loan maximum of $150,000 limits access for businesses requiring larger financing
Limited digital presence compared to neobanks; website does not advertise online account opening
No mention of checking account features, minimum balance requirements, or fee structures
Geographically constrained by local branch network (specific locations not detailed on main pages)
Website does not provide current rates for consumer or business lending products beyond CDs
best_for: Small business owners seeking SBA loans up to $150,000 with relationship-based lending
Savers and retirees looking for competitive CD rates with AARP-certified elder protections
Customers in UMWSB service areas wanting comprehensive banking (lending + deposits) from one institution
Borrowers prioritizing local branch relationships and personalized service over digital-only banking
diagnosis: United Midwest Savings Bank is best for business owners, savers, and borrowers who value relationship-based banking with comprehensive services (lending plus deposits) and community accountability. The main caveat is that borrowers needing SBA loans exceeding $150,000, customers outside their branch footprint, or those prioritizing 24/7 digital banking will need to evaluate alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
